seoTitle: How to Create a Popup in Power BI (Overlay Trick)
metaDescription: Build an interactive popup overlay in Power BI to show extra detail without cluttering your dashboard, using this step-by-step approach.
intro: Popups let you show extra detail in Power BI, like a metric breakdown, without permanently taking up space on your dashboard. This guide explains how to build an interactive overlay that appears on demand using bookmarks and buttons. It's a useful technique for anyone who wants richer reports without overcrowding the main view.

steps.heading: Enable popups on your report
steps.body: Start by setting up the report so popup-style overlays can be triggered, which is the foundation for the interactive effect.

steps.heading: Build the overlay content
steps.body: Create the visuals or details that will appear inside the popup, keeping them focused on the extra context you want to reveal.

steps.heading: Create the interactive overlay
steps.body: Use bookmarks and a trigger element so the overlay appears on top of the report when a user interacts with it, then disappears when closed.

steps.heading: Enhance the user experience
steps.body: Refine the popup's positioning and styling so it feels like a natural part of the report rather than an awkward addition.

steps.heading: Apply it to a business use case
steps.body: Use the popup to show something practical, such as detailed metrics or added context, so viewers get more insight without extra clutter.

faq.question: Do I need any add-ins to create popups in Power BI?
faq.answer: No, this overlay technique is built using standard Power BI features like bookmarks and buttons, without needing extra add-ins.

faq.question: Can I use popups to show detailed metrics on click?
faq.answer: Yes, that's one of the most common uses, letting viewers click to reveal detailed metrics without cluttering the main dashboard.

faq.question: Will popups work the same way when the report is shared?
faq.answer: Yes, since the interactivity is built into the report itself, it works the same for anyone viewing the shared report.

faq.question: Can I have multiple different popups on one report page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set up several overlays as long as each one is tied to its own bookmark and trigger.

faq.question: Is this popup technique difficult for beginners?
faq.answer: It's approachable for beginners since it relies on standard bookmark and button features rather than custom code.
